rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex for AGREE expressions.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reside in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of the referenced elements.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure type consistency between assumed expressions and their actual declarations in AGREE.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as AGREE variables.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
Implementation-level subcomponent paths in type contracts
